INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 21 Apr 2026, 6:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
According to the SCCC, a group of unknown individuals illegally entered the plant premises and were spotted moving inside the area with the intent to identify and collect scrap materials.
Based on information received from the SCCC regarding unauthorized entry, the on-duty guard proceeded to the location. The suspects escaped upon sensing the guard’s arrival, abandoning collected scrap material valued at Rs. 1,800 at the site.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at VAL J site at 22 Apr 2026, 10:45 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1045 hrs, the Siriapalli Supervisor reported that five villagers from Kumada Palli had gathered at the Siriapalli New Road area and stopped the movement of ash vehicles due to dust-related concerns. The information was promptly shared with the CSR, Ash Team, and SCR. After resolution of the issue, ash vehicle movement resumed at around 1706 hrs.
RCA-"Community dissatisfaction due to dust pollution issues.
Lack of proactive stakeholder engagement with nearby villages.
Insufficient dust control measures (water sprinkling/road maintenance)."

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at VAB site at 11 May 2026, 6:55 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Yesterday at 18:55 hrs, Mr. Gokuldas Govekar, a resident of Navelim Village, blocked the C Gate In with his two-wheeler. He stated that his tipper vehicle, bearing registration number GA04T4036, has been blocked for the past two months. According to him, the vehicle was blocked under the instructions of CSR Head Mr. Prasad Asgaonkar. He said that he would not leave the gate area until his vehicle was unblocked.
Mr. Gokul Das appeared to be under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident. Our security team spoke with him, counseled him, and explained the situation. After discussion, he agreed to remove his two-wheeler from the gate area.
At 19:23 hrs, the gate was successfully cleared, and normal transport movement resumed.

INCIDENTTITLE: "On 12.05.2026 at around 0830 hrs, Chitradurga G-Shift vehicles were intercepted at Kadleguddu village by an individual named Maruthi  .
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"On 12.05.2026 at around 0830 hrs, Chitradurga G-Shift vehicles were intercepted at Kadleguddu village by an individual named Maruthi and a group of local residents. The protesters halted the shift vehicles to demand employment opportunities, service contracts, and the initiation of community projects under CSR funding. In response, the ER/PR and other relevant departments were immediately notified to coordinate corrective actions, and the Seregire police were contacted for assistance. 
A formal complaint was lodged via the 112 emergency line at 0848 hrs, and police team arrived on the scene by 0948 hrs to begin negotiations with the villagers. Following a joint discussion involving the 112 police, the internal security team, and the local community, a temporary resolution was reached, allowing the shift vehicles to be released and resume operations at 1005 hrs."

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 01 Apr 2026, 10:35 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 2235 hours, the CCTV operator observed and reported that a group comprising 8–10 individuals, with suspected intent to commit theft, had assembled outside Garga Yard-1, following which four individuals gained unauthorized access to the yard. 
Upon receiving the information, the Security Shift I/C, along with the QRT team, immediately reached the location. Upon sighting the security team, the thieves fled from the yard. The Security Shift I/C and team remain present in the yard for approximately two hours. Subsequently, while unable to gain entry, the miscreants pelted stones from outside for a brief period. The security team effectively handled the situation, leading to the miscreants retreating from the area. No injuries or loss of property were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAL J site at 15 Apr 2026, 3:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1520 hrs, during routine checking at the Main Gate, Kapila Kisan (Gate Pass No. 6600017782/10/00019), Designation: Helper, Contractor: RKTC Infratech Limited, was found attempting to enter the premises with an expired gate pass.
RCA-Non-compliance with punch procedures due to misunderstanding or deliberate bypass of access control rules, coupled with gaps in awareness and enforcement of movement protocols between multiple gates/locations.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 18 Apr 2026, 12:56 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0056 hrs, information was received from P-6 Alfa (Hathi Side) regarding suspicious movement. Upon receipt of the information, the IPP QRT was immediately mobilized to the location, and the SCR was informed accordingly. Subsequently, the Baaz Team, NDO, Plant-2 QRT, and BOP QRT also reached the site. A joint search operation was conducted by all responding teams. During inspection, it was observed that the perimeter fencing had been cut, indicating suspected unauthorized entry. A detailed search was also conducted outside the boundary wall, during which approximately 100 kg of iron scrap material was recovered.
RCA-"Perimeter fencing vulnerability (easy to cut and access).
Absence of intrusion detection systems (IDS/PIDS) in boundary area.
Delayed detection, allowing scrap movement outside boundary."

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAL J site at 19 Apr 2026, 2:35 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at IRON ORE GOA site at 05 Apr 2026, 3:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 05.04.2026 at around 15:20 hrs, during internal material shifting, a loaded transport truck (H-No. 655 / GA 01 U 9585) driven by Mr. Jamuna toppled at the 1Top area road.

The information was received from Morning Shift Incharge Mr. Kartik Naik. Immediately, Security informed the Control Room, Logistics, and Ambulance services.

Security and the Logistics team reached the spot promptly. The driver had safely exited the vehicle on his own. The ambulance arrived at 15:26 hrs and shifted the driver to PHC Bicholim at 15:28 hrs.

As per Logistics Shift Incharge Mr. Aditya Mishra, the driver is in stable condition with minor fracture only. The vehicle sustained minor damage (front windshield broken).

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at MEL site at 11 May 2026, 7:05 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 11th May 2026 at around 0615hrs, a theft incident was unfoiled at the Main Gate during routine frisking procedures. Mr. Ramesh, Fire Supervisor was found concealing a fan motor housing along with the motor assembly and tried to unlawfully take the material away from the premises.

During standard Gate exit checks, the on-duty Security Officer and Security Guard observed suspicious behaviour and conducted a thorough frisking and inspection of the individual’s belongings. Upon inspection, the concealed item was discovered hidden in a manner intended to evade detection.

The security officer immediately detained the individual and informed the Lead Security. The material was recovered in full, and no loss to the company property occurred. Preliminary inquiry indicates deliberate intent by Mr. Ramesh to commit theft by concealing the item. 

The individual statement was taken, and Gate Pass was collected from him and sent outside the Plant Premises. Further Lead Security went to the incident scene for checking the area. It was found that a Fan was replaced in recent past (March Month) view repair and only the fan blades were found at the Fire store. It was ascertained from the other workforce working at fire office that the Motor along with the Housing is missing today.  

The issue was highlighted to higher authorities for further investigation and necessary disciplinary/legal action.

According to him, the vehicle was blocked under the instructions of CSR Head Mr. Prasad Asgaonkar. He said that he would not leave the gate area until his vehicle was unblocked.
Mr. Gokul Das appeared to be under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident. Our security team spoke with him, counseled him, and explained the situation. After discussion, he agreed to remove his two-wheeler from the gate area.
At 19:23 hrs, the gate was successfully cleared, and normal transport movement resumed.

INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 14.05.2026 at around 1125 hrs, a group of women from Meghalahalli village staged a blocking the main gate of the Administration Block, demanding the provision of a drinking water borewell in their village.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unsafe Condition at RDC site at 11 Apr 2026, 12:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 11.04.2026, at around 12:45 hrs, an incident occurred at Weighbridge-B near the heavy vehicle checkout post. Vehicle No. RJ47GA3975, belonging to Saktopaya Logistics Private Limited and driven by Mr. Jaswant Jatiya (DL No. RJ0920200003704), hit Vehicle No. RJ09GE2066, which was properly standing in the queue for weighment. The incident occurred while the driver of Vehicle No. RJ47GA3975 was attempting to get the vehicle weighed at the weighbridge. Due to the absence of a tag, the driver repeatedly reversed the vehicle and struck the vehicle standing behind. The collision occurred due to improper reversing without ensuring safe clearance. As a result of the collision, the front portion of Vehicle No. RJ09GE2066 was damaged. Statements from both drivers were recorded, and an alcohol check was conducted, which was found negative. Driver Mr. Jaswant Jatiya was sent to HZL Hospital for a drug test, which returned positive for OPI. An email has been sent to the concerned department for further action.
